notes: Backfill batch — reinsurers, adjacent/strategic, brokers, additional lenders/operators. ~115 companies targeting 300 total.

companies.name: Everest Re Group
companies.description: Largest Bermuda-based reinsurer with $12.9B GWP in 2024. Writes specialty casualty, property, and professional lines globally.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Top-tier Bermuda reinsurer with large casualty book and appetite for specialty risk. DIAIC's $3.465B ceded reinsurance need is squarely in Everest's wheelhouse.

companies.name: RenaissanceRe Holdings
companies.description: Bermuda-based reinsurer with $11.7B premiums in 2024. Specializes in property catastrophe and specialty reinsurance.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Large specialty reinsurer with active interest in novel risk classes. Could anchor a treaty for GPU residual value risk.

companies.name: Arch Capital Group
companies.description: Bermuda specialty insurer/reinsurer with $11.1B GWP. Active across casualty, property, mortgage, and specialty.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Arch has a strong appetite for specialty casualty and equipment-related risks. Somers Re subsidiary also relevant.

companies.name: PartnerRe
companies.description: Global reinsurer with $9.3B in premiums, owned by Covéa. Strong specialty casualty and technology risk capabilities.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Global specialty reinsurer with tech and casualty expertise. Covéa ownership provides capital stability for large capacity deals.

companies.name: AXIS Capital
companies.description: Bermuda specialty insurer/reinsurer with $2.4B in reinsurance premiums. Operates Lloyd's Syndicate 1686 and has dedicated technology insurance division.

key_contacts.name: Lauren Kent
key_contacts.title: Head of Global Casualty & Specialty, AXIS Re

companies.corgi_fit_rationale: AXIS Technology Insurance division makes them a highly relevant reinsurance partner. Both Bermuda and Lloyd's platforms available for capacity.

companies.name: Convex Group
companies.description: Specialty (re)insurer with $5.2B GWP in 2024. Recently launched Lloyd's Syndicate 1984 writing casualty, crisis management, marine, property.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Fast-growing specialty reinsurer with Lloyd's platform. Casualty and specialty focus aligns with GPU RVG. Syndicate 1984 actively seeking novel risk.

companies.name: Hamilton Insurance Group
companies.description: Bermuda specialty insurer/reinsurer writing property, casualty, and specialty lines globally.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Mid-size specialty reinsurer with appetite for innovative risk. Relevant for treaty or quota share participation.

companies.name: Fidelis Insurance Group
companies.description: Bermuda specialty insurer/reinsurer focusing on Specialty, Bespoke, and Reinsurance pillars. Known for agile underwriting of complex risks.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Fidelis excels in bespoke/complex risk — GPU residual value reinsurance fits their mandate. Strong relationships with Fidelis Partnership MGA structure.

companies.name: Conduit Re
companies.description: Bermuda pure-play reinsurer writing property, casualty, and specialty. Class of 2020 company targeting mid-teens RoE.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Pure reinsurance focus with clean casualty and specialty book. Seeking differentiated risk in their treaty portfolio.

companies.name: Skyward Specialty Insurance
companies.description: US specialty insurer with nine underwriting divisions including Captives, Credit/Ag Reinsurance, and Professional Lines. Owns Skyward Re captive.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Specialty programs division could support GPU RVG. Captive expertise relevant to DIAIC structure. Captives division underwriter worth approaching.

companies.name: Nephila Capital (Markel)
companies.description: World's largest ILS and catastrophe reinsurance asset manager, owned by Markel. Manages $10B+ in ILS capacity.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: ILS capital sources could provide alternative capacity for GPU risk. Markel umbrella gives access to broader reinsurance relationships.

companies.name: HSB Group (Hartford Steam Boiler)
companies.description: Munich Re subsidiary specializing in equipment breakdown reinsurance. Provides commercial reinsurance for technology equipment, boilers, and machinery.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: HSB is THE equipment breakdown reinsurance specialist globally. GPU residual value risk is directly adjacent to their core competency. Munich Re parent gives massive capacity.

companies.name: Beazley plc
companies.description: Lloyd's Syndicate 2623 with £2.3B stamp capacity. Specialty lines including cyber, technology, marine, and property. One of largest Lloyd's syndicates.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Beazley's technology E&O and cyber capabilities make them highly relevant. Could reinsure DIAIC's GPU RVG book. Active in specialty risk innovation.

companies.name: Brit Insurance (Syndicate 2987)
companies.description: Lloyd's Syndicate 2987. Specialty insurer/reinsurer owned by Fairfax Financial. Active in property, casualty, marine, and technology.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Brit's casualty and specialty book includes technology equipment risks. Lloyd's platform relevant for capacity in specialty programs.

companies.name: SCOR SE
companies.description: Major global reinsurer with specialty insurance solutions at Lloyd's. Known for cyber and technology E&O focus at Lloyd's.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: SCOR has a key market position in Cyber and Technology E&O at Lloyd's. Specialty insurance solutions division directly relevant to GPU risk.

companies.name: Somers Re (Arch Capital)
companies.description: Arch Capital-relaunched specialty reinsurer (formerly Watford Re). Combines underwriting and investment expertise.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Total return reinsurer model. Specialty and casualty focus. Part of Arch Capital ecosystem — relevant alongside main Arch Re relationship.

companies.name: Transatlantic Holdings (TransRe)
companies.description: Alleghany subsidiary (now Berkshire Hathaway). Global reinsurer writing casualty, property, and specialty treaty business.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Large casualty treaty reinsurer with Berkshire Hathaway capital behind it. Could participate in a multi-reinsurer program for GPU RVG.

companies.name: General Re (Gen Re)
companies.description: Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary. One of world's largest property/casualty reinsurers. Strong US casualty treaty market presence.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Berkshire capital backing means virtually unlimited capacity for structured risks. Casualty treaty group worth approaching for GPU RVG reinsurance.

companies.name: W.R. Berkley Corporation
companies.description: US specialty insurer/reinsurer with multiple operating units. Berkley Re is the reinsurance division. Known for innovation in specialty lines.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Berkley's specialty units actively seek differentiated risk. Multiple operating companies could participate at different layers of GPU RVG program.

companies.name: Tokio Marine Group
companies.description: Japan's largest insurer/reinsurer. Operates internationally through Tokio Marine Kiln (TMK) Lloyd's platform and Tokio Marine America.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Large Asian reinsurer with strong specialty lines globally through TMK. US and Lloyd's platforms both relevant for DIAIC reinsurance capacity.

companies.name: MS&AD Insurance Group (Mitsui Sumitomo Re)
companies.description: Major Japanese insurance group with reinsurance operations via Mitsui Sumitomo Re and InterAmerican. One of Japan's largest re/insurers.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Japanese insurer with appetite for specialty treaty. Asian angle relevant if Corgi expands to Asia-Pacific GPU operators.

companies.name: Sompo International
companies.description: Bermuda-based specialty insurer/reinsurer, subsidiary of Sompo Holdings Japan. Active in casualty, property, and specialty lines.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Well-capitalized Bermuda reinsurer with specialty focus. Sompo Japan parent provides significant capacity. Good fit for structured GPU risk.

companies.name: Aspen Insurance Holdings
companies.description: Bermuda specialty insurer/reinsurer. Active across casualty, property, marine, and specialty lines. Strong Lloyd's and Bermuda presence.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Active specialty reinsurer with technology insurance expertise. Bermuda and Lloyd's platforms available for large structured programs.

companies.name: Hiscox Re
companies.description: Specialty insurer/reinsurer operating through Lloyd's and Bermuda. Known for complex specialty risks and casualty reinsurance.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Hiscox is a proven innovator in specialty casualty. Known for willingness to underwrite novel risks. Good reinsurance candidate for GPU RVG capacity.

companies.name: Markel Corporation
companies.description: Specialty insurer/reinsurer holding company. Owns Nephila, Markel Re, and various specialty MGAs. $16B+ in premium.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Markel's specialty insurance and Nephila ILS arms both relevant. Markel Ventures' tech investment focus aligns with GPU infrastructure.

companies.name: Enstar Group
companies.description: Bermuda-based specialty run-off and reinsurance. Active in acquiring and managing complex specialty programs.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: While primarily run-off focused, Enstar can provide alternative capital structures for novel insurance programs. Could assist with residual value program structuring.

companies.name: Lancashire Holdings
companies.description: Bermuda specialty insurer/reinsurer. Actively underwriting property, casualty, and specialty short-tail lines. Known for disciplined cycle management.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Specialty reinsurer comfortable with complex structured products. Could participate in GPU RVG reinsurance at competitive terms.

companies.name: Odyssey Re (Fairfax Financial)
companies.description: Fairfax Financial subsidiary. Global reinsurer with US, Europe, and London platforms. Property, casualty, and specialty.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Fairfax capital backing. Active in specialty casualty and technology coverage. Good candidate for facultative or treaty participation.

companies.name: Qatar Re
companies.description: Bermuda-domiciled reinsurer backed by Qatar Insurance Company. Active in specialty lines globally.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Middle East capital source for reinsurance. Relevant if Corgi targets Gulf-region GPU operators. Qatar sovereign backing provides stable long-term capacity.

companies.name: Greenlight Re
companies.description: Cayman Islands specialty reinsurer with David Einhorn's investment approach. Active in casualty and specialty.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Specialty reinsurer that seeks differentiated risk/return profiles. GPU RVG could appeal as a non-correlated risk.

companies.name: Third Point Re
companies.description: Bermuda specialty reinsurer with investment portfolio managed by Third Point LLC. Focuses on property and casualty.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Hedge fund-backed reinsurer that seeks alpha from both underwriting and investments. GPU risk as asset class could appeal to investment committee.

companies.name: RVI Group
companies.description: Specialist RVI (Residual Value Insurance) reinsurer. Provides reinsurance capacity for RVI programs globally. Direct comp/partner for DIAIC.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: RVI Group IS the market for residual value reinsurance. They provide treaty reinsurance for RVI programs. Critical potential reinsurance partner for DIAIC. Must contact.

companies.name: Specialty MGA UK
companies.description: UK-based specialty MGA delivering RVI and RVG solutions for diverse asset classes including high-value equipment. Direct comp and potential reinsurance/co-insurance partner.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: One of the few specialty MGAs doing RVG/RVI for equipment. Could be a reinsurance partner, capacity provider, or competitor intelligence source. High priority.

companies.name: Matrix Specialty Underwriting
companies.description: Specialty MGA focused on RVI for diverse asset classes. Leading the RVI sector toward broader functionality including industrial equipment and capital-raising applications.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Matrix is explicitly expanding RVI to new asset classes. GPU/AI hardware is a natural next category. Potential reinsurance capacity provider or co-underwriter.

companies.name: Asset RVI
companies.description: Specialist RVI provider with deep expertise in asset residual value insurance across equipment, technology, and commercial assets.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Direct specialist in RVI for equipment assets. Natural reinsurance or co-insurance partner for GPU RVG program. Must research and contact.

companies.name: Allianz Re
companies.description: Allianz Group's global reinsurance arm. Recently partnered with Oaktree to establish Lloyd's Syndicate 1890. Large capacity across all lines.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Massive capacity. New Oaktree partnership (Syndicate 1890) shows appetite for innovative structures. Allianz's tech sector expertise relevant.

companies.name: AIG Syndicate 2478 at Lloyd's
companies.description: AIG's new Lloyd's reinsurance syndicate launched January 2025 with Blackstone strategic backing. Focuses on reinsurance lines.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: New Lloyd's reinsurance syndicate actively building book. AIG's technology insurance capabilities and Blackstone's infrastructure background align with GPU risk.

companies.name: Tokio Marine Kiln (TMK)
companies.description: Lloyd's managing agent owned by Tokio Marine Group. One of largest Lloyd's syndicates writing complex specialty, property, and casualty.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: TMK's broad specialty mandate and large Lloyd's capacity make them relevant for GPU RVG reinsurance. Technology insurance team worth approaching.

companies.name: Apollo Underwriting
companies.description: Lloyd's managing agency writing specialty property, liability, and reinsurance. Part of Apollo Global Management ecosystem.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Apollo Global Management connection means access to significant alternative capital. Lloyd's specialty platform relevant for GPU risk capacity.

companies.name: Guy Carpenter
companies.description: World's leading reinsurance intermediary, Marsh McLennan subsidiary. Manages $18B+ in reinsurance placements globally.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Primary reinsurance broker for placing DIAIC's $3.465B ceded reinsurance. Guy Carpenter's specialty team would lead structuring and placement. Critical relationship.

companies.name: Gallagher Re
companies.description: Global reinsurance broker, subsidiary of Arthur J. Gallagher. $13.5B acquisition of AssuredPartners in progress, expanding specialty capabilities.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Major reinsurance broker with global specialty practice. Can place GPU RVG reinsurance across Bermuda and Lloyd's markets.

companies.name: Howden Re
companies.description: Global reinsurance broker from Howden Group. Known for innovative placement strategies and specialty risk expertise.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Howden Re focuses on specialty and innovative placements. Strong Lloyd's relationships. Could structure and place GPU RVG reinsurance program.

companies.name: BMS Re
companies.description: Independent specialist reinsurance broker. Recently named Latin America and Caribbean CEO. Known for specialty and casualty reinsurance.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Independent reinsurance broker with specialty focus. BMS Re's casualty and specialty practice could place GPU RVG in Lloyd's/Bermuda market.

companies.name: Miller Insurance
companies.description: Independent specialist (re)insurance broker known for complex specialty risk. Strong Lloyd's and international market relationships.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Independent specialist broker with deep Lloyd's access. Can place niche programs that larger brokers may not prioritize.

companies.name: Lockton Re
companies.description: Reinsurance division of Lockton, world's largest private insurance broker. Strong specialty casualty capabilities.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Lockton Re's specialty casualty team can place programs in both US and international markets. Private ownership allows flexibility.

companies.name: TigerRisk Partners (Howden Tiger)
companies.description: Specialty reinsurance broker now part of Howden Group. Known for capital markets, cat bonds, and specialty placement.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Capital markets expertise from TigerRisk + Howden's specialty focus. Could structure ILS or cat bond alternative for GPU depreciation risk.

companies.name: Newfront Insurance
companies.description: Modern tech-forward insurance brokerage. Raised $200M+. Serves tech companies and digital infrastructure clients.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Tech-focused broker with strong ties to VC-backed AI/GPU companies. Well-positioned to introduce Corgi's RVG to tech clients.

companies.name: Woodruff Sawyer
companies.description: Top independent insurance brokerage focused on technology, life sciences, and private equity. Strong Bay Area/tech client base.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Strong relationships with tech companies, GPU cloud operators, and PE-backed infrastructure firms. Can introduce Corgi to GPU operator clients.

companies.name: AssuredPartners
companies.description: Large independent insurance broker. Pending acquisition by Arthur J. Gallagher. Strong mid-market and specialty capabilities.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Large broker with specialty capabilities and wide industry coverage. Post-Gallagher acquisition could access Gallagher Re placement infrastructure.

companies.name: Amwins Group
companies.description: Largest wholesale insurance broker in the US. Specializes in placing complex, unusual, and high-risk coverages in Lloyd's and E&S markets.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Major E&S/wholesale broker with Lloyd's access. Can distribute GPU RVG product to a wide range of retail broker clients seeking coverage for clients.

companies.name: Ryan Specialty
companies.description: Leading US specialty insurance MGA/wholesale broker. Specializes in complex risks not in standard markets.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Ryan Specialty's MGU/specialty focus means they can create and distribute novel products like GPU RVG. Could act as program administrator or placement broker.

companies.name: Gallagher (Equipment Finance Division)
companies.description: Arthur J. Gallagher's technology insurance practice. Provides specialist technology sector coverage and equipment finance-related insurance.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: AJG's tech insurance practice serves data centers and AI companies. Bridge between insurance (RVG product) and equipment finance clients needing collateral protection.

companies.name: SLYD Finance
companies.description: Specialty GPU hardware financing for enterprises. Structures deals from $500K–$50M. Works with Dell, HPE, Supermicro, Lenovo, Gigabyte.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Pure-play GPU financing company. RVG would directly de-risk their loan portfolio by guaranteeing collateral floor at maturity. Highly motivated prospect.

companies.name: Vertical Data
companies.description: Long-term asset-backed GPU financing for enterprises, AI startups, and cloud providers. Terms up to 36–60 months backed by hardware.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: GPU-collateralized lending with multi-year terms is exactly where RVG protects lenders. Vertical Data would benefit from RVG as collateral enhancement tool.

companies.name: USD.AI
companies.description: Stablecoin protocol for AI infrastructure financing. Raised $13M Series A. Provides fast credit to AI companies using GPU collateral. Tokenized GPU lending.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Crypto-native GPU lending uses GPU residual value as collateral. RVG would provide the floor guarantee needed for tokenized GPU loan structures.

companies.name: GPU Financing (gpufinancing.com)
companies.description: Dedicated AI company helping startups and enterprises secure GPU capital and hardware. Specializes in equipment financing for AI infrastructure.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Pure GPU financing specialist. RVG would enable them to offer larger loan amounts and longer terms with guaranteed collateral backing.

companies.name: SQN Capital Management
companies.description: Equipment leasing and finance investment manager. Runs public and private equipment finance funds. Diverse equipment asset classes.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Specialty equipment finance fund manager. GPU-backed loans would benefit from RVG floor guarantee. Could be a distribution partner for RVG bundled into loans.

companies.name: Wintrust Equipment Finance
companies.description: Wintrust Financial's equipment finance division. Provides lease and loan financing for technology and specialty equipment.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Bank-owned equipment finance with technology vertical. RVG improves LTV ratios on AI GPU loans, allowing Wintrust to compete more aggressively.

companies.name: Marlin Capital Solutions
companies.description: Equipment financing specialist focused on small and mid-size businesses. Technology and AI equipment vertical.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: SMB-focused equipment lender moving into AI hardware. RVG could help manage residual value risk on GPU loans for smaller AI companies.

companies.name: Crestmark (Meta Financial Group)
companies.description: Specialty commercial finance focusing on equipment leasing, working capital, and government lending. Technology and AI hardware focus growing.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Specialty equipment lessor with growing tech focus. RVG enables better LTV ratios on technology equipment portfolios.

companies.name: Plus500 Capital (AI infrastructure)
companies.description: Emerging AI infrastructure financing vehicle. Provides capital to AI/GPU cloud operators through asset-backed structures.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Newer infrastructure lender specifically targeting GPU cloud sector. RVG directly addresses their core underwriting challenge — residual value uncertainty.

companies.name: Pacific Western Equipment Finance
companies.description: Bank-owned equipment finance division. Technology and digital infrastructure lending. Part of Pacific Western Bank.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Bank equipment finance with West Coast tech exposure. RVG could be bundled into GPU lending program.

companies.name: North Mill Equipment Finance
companies.description: Specialty equipment finance company focused on technology and advanced manufacturing. Works with emerging technology companies.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Tech-focused equipment finance with appetite for emerging hardware categories including AI accelerators.

companies.name: Banc of California Equipment Finance
companies.description: California bank with equipment finance capabilities. Strong relationships with tech companies in California.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: West Coast bank with tech company client base. Could introduce RVG to GPU operator clients needing to optimize capital structure.

companies.name: Equinix
companies.description: World's largest data center REIT. 507,000+ interconnections across 77 metros. Critical AI/GPU infrastructure backbone.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Equinix hosts many GPU cloud operators. As infrastructure owner, they care about hardware value protection. Could co-market RVG to colocation tenants.

companies.name: Digital Realty Trust
companies.description: World's largest data center platform. 300+ facilities in 50+ cities. Key AI/GPU infrastructure host.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Largest data center landlord with extensive GPU tenant base. Partnership could give Corgi access to hundreds of GPU operators.

companies.name: EdgeCore Digital Infrastructure
companies.description: Specialized high-density AI data center provider. Secured $1.9B debt financing in 2024. Focuses on hyperscale and AI workloads.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: AI-specific data center operator with significant GPU hardware exposure. RVG directly protects their GPU capex. Active financing means lender relationships also relevant.

companies.name: Iron Mountain (Data Center + ITAD)
companies.description: Data center REIT and ITAD provider. Acquired Wisetek ITAD in 2024. Manages hardware disposition for major enterprises.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Dual role: data center operator (GPU exposure) and ITAD (secondary market). RVG affects both how they value owned hardware and what they pay for disposed assets.

companies.name: Sims Lifecycle Services
companies.description: Global IT asset disposition leader. 21% revenue growth H1 FY2025. Repurposed 4.5M units (80% increase) from data center GPU demand.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Major ITAD with booming GPU data center business. RVG affects residual values in their market. Partnership could enable better GPU pricing data and ecosystem integration.

companies.name: SK Tes (TES Global)
companies.description: Largest ITAD company by global R2-certified facilities (31 sites). Recently scaled data center ITAD business. Handles GPU disposition at scale.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Leading ITAD with specific data center GPU focus. As the de facto end-of-life handler for GPU hardware, their pricing data is critical to RVG market. Strategic data partnership potential.

companies.name: ALTA Technologies
companies.description: R2v3 certified buyer and seller of used/refurbished NVIDIA H100 and A100 GPUs. Serves AI, LLM, and advanced computing environments.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Core GPU secondary market participant. RVG floor guarantees the minimum value they'd pay for GPUs. Strong candidate for integration with SiliconNavigator pricing data.

companies.name: NetEquity.com
companies.description: Competitive buyback, trade-in, and consignment remarketing of used and excess Nvidia GPU/datacenter hardware including H100 and A100.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Used GPU remarketer whose business depends on accurate residual value forecasting. RVG ecosystem data and partnerships directly support their business model.

companies.name: Silicon Data (SiliconNavigator)
companies.description: GPU pricing and market intelligence platform. SiliconNavigator™ covers 80% of H100 pricing. Raised $4.7M seed from DRW and Jump Trading (March 2025).
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: SiliconNavigator is already cited in Corgi's product knowledge as providing 80% H100 coverage for secondary market pricing. Direct data partnership or integration would strengthen RVG underwriting.

companies.name: Introl
companies.description: AI hardware infrastructure company providing secondary market services and market intelligence for GPU assets. Focus on enterprise GPU lifecycle optimization.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: GPU secondary market intelligence and lifecycle optimization. Introl's customers face exactly the depreciation risk RVG addresses.

companies.name: exIT Technologies
companies.description: IT asset disposition specialist with GPU/graphics card liquidation services. Handles enterprise data center hardware refresh and disposition.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: ITAD with specific GPU focus. Their GPU pricing data and buyer/seller network is directly relevant to RVG floor value calculation and claim settlement.

companies.name: Back Market
companies.description: Global marketplace for refurbished electronics including certified pre-owned AMD and NVIDIA graphics cards. Major consumer and prosumer GPU secondary market.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Consumer-grade GPU secondary market; less relevant for enterprise H100/A100. However, their pricing data contributes to overall GPU residual value signals.

companies.name: SellGPU.com
companies.description: Founded 2014. Purchases GPUs, CPUs, RAM, SSDs, server CPUs, and bulk IT assets. Instant quotes for GPU liquidation.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Spot market GPU buyer. Their bid prices represent the floor of the secondary market. Relevant for RVG claim valuation methodology.

companies.name: GPUnex
companies.description: Operational GPU compute marketplace with 2,400+ active GPUs across 150+ verified data centers. Rental and investment marketplace.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: GPU rental marketplace provides real-time utilization and pricing data. RVG would attract larger GPU hardware owners to list and insure their fleets.

companies.name: Vast.ai
companies.description: Decentralized GPU rental marketplace. Connects GPU providers with AI researchers. 40+ data centers, 20,000+ GPUs. On-demand compute.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: GPU marketplace operator with GPU hardware owners as customers. RVG would help hardware providers on Vast.ai protect the value of their investment.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.description: AI-focused GPU cloud. Closed $7.5B Blackstone debt facility 2024. IPO in 2025. $18.8B total GPU-backed debt using NVIDIA H100s as collateral.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: CoreWeave is THE largest GPU-collateralized debt issuer. $18.8B exposure to GPU residual values. RVG would reduce their cost of capital and enable higher LTV on GPU-backed debt.

companies.name: Lambda Labs
companies.description: AI-focused GPU cloud provider. Executed $1.5B sale-leaseback with NVIDIA. Major GPU owner/operator.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Large GPU cloud with significant hardware ownership. Sale-leaseback model shows active capital structure optimization. RVG would guarantee residual value in their hardware portfolio.

companies.name: Together AI
companies.description: AI cloud platform for training and inference. Owns significant GPU infrastructure. Raised $305M Series B.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Well-funded AI GPU operator with large capex commitment. RVG hedges depreciation risk on GPU fleet. Series B funding means active capital management.

companies.name: Lepton AI
companies.description: AI-focused cloud platform founded by former Bytedance and Microsoft AI researchers. GPU infrastructure provider.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: GPU cloud operator with hardware ownership. Relevant prospect for RVG as they scale GPU fleet.

companies.name: Ori Cloud
companies.description: UK-based AI cloud provider. Specializes in on-demand GPU compute for training and inference.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: European GPU cloud operator with hardware capex. RVG hedges their hardware depreciation in European market.

companies.name: Cirrascale Cloud Services
companies.description: GPU-accelerated cloud for AI/ML. Operates NVIDIA DGX and HGX systems. Serves AI researchers and enterprises.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Specialized GPU cloud with significant NVIDIA hardware investment. RVG protects value of DGX/HGX systems which depreciate rapidly.

companies.name: Sharktech
companies.description: DDoS-protected dedicated server and GPU cloud provider. Operates US data centers with AI GPU infrastructure.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Mid-size GPU cloud operator with owned hardware. RVG relevant for their GPU fleet depreciation management.

companies.name: DataCrunch
companies.description: European GPU cloud provider. High-performance NVIDIA GPU instances for AI/ML. Data center presence in Finland.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: EU-based GPU cloud with owned hardware. Relevant as Corgi considers international expansion.

companies.name: Novita AI
companies.description: AI cloud platform offering GPU instances and model APIs. Growing GPU infrastructure footprint.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Newer GPU cloud operator building hardware base. RVG valuable as they build investment case for GPU acquisition.

companies.name: Prime Intellect
companies.description: Decentralized AI training platform. Aggregates GPU compute across providers for large distributed training runs.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Emerging GPU aggregation platform. GPU hardware owners in their network face depreciation risk RVG addresses.

companies.name: Nebius Group
companies.description: European AI cloud infrastructure company spun out of Yandex. Major GPU data center investments in Europe and US.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Well-funded EU GPU cloud operator with large hardware capex. International GPU operators are strategic targets for Corgi. Good fit for RVG.

companies.name: Nscale (Norwegian GPU Cloud)
companies.description: European GPU cloud focused on sustainable AI compute. Significant NVIDIA GPU hardware purchases. Norway-based data centers.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: European GPU cloud with significant NVIDIA hardware commitment. RVG hedges depreciation risk on large GPU fleet.

companies.name: Replicate
companies.description: AI model deployment platform running on GPU infrastructure. Provides API access to AI models via owned/operated GPU infrastructure.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: GPU-intensive platform with hardware exposure. RVG relevant as they scale GPU fleet for model inference.

companies.name: Perplexity AI (Infrastructure)
companies.description: AI search engine with significant owned GPU infrastructure. $520M raised. Building large-scale GPU fleet for inference.
companies.corgi_fit_rationale: Well-funded AI company building owned GPU fleet. As they scale hardware investment, RVG becomes valuable depreciation hedge.

companies.name: SambaNova Systems
